published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022: sharp Riesz omitted-variable-bias support bounds for broad linear causal functionals, including policy effects

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: sharp joint-misspecification variational interval for offline policy value is nearby but not the CH--Riesz minimax-regret/Tikhonov policy formula

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 give sharp/robust MSM policy learning, not a CH--Riesz partial-R2 Tikhonov minimax frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: compatibility/non-rectangularity for joint misspecification, not CH--Riesz boundary laws or MSM/AC bridge limits

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022 already has sharp Riesz OVB boundary logic for fixed functionals; KallusZhou2021 and AdjahoChristensen2025 supply the separate limiting faces

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The support-bound kernel is already supplied by ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022; the proposal correctly demotes it to calibration, but per-theorem novelty is not new.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The CH--Riesz budget is not a precise mathematical restriction: sigma_CH is left as an observed-scale/Riesz scale placeholder, and R2_Q(Delta,U | X,A) is ambiguous for arbitrary latent U.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters | Theorem 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Q_CH is said to match P while also carrying a covariate-shift budget delta; unless the target law or support-function add-on is part of V_Q, the delta Sigma_X term and AC limit are not a well-defined identified-regret functional.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Section 4 advertises an iff policy-regret compatibility frontier, but Conjecture 2 states endpoint-attaining boundary laws and bridge limits, not an iff compatibility criterion.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the informal CH--Riesz budget with explicit variance/partial-R2 equations matching CCNSS notation, including the exact definition of sigma_CH and the denominator scales for DeltaY and DeltaR_h.

Separate latent-law matching from target-population shift: define either an augmented target law Q^target with shifted X marginal or define R_CH as the CH regret plus an external support-function penalty, then restate Theorem 1 and the AC sanity check accordingly.

Make the advertised iff frontier a formal statement: specify the primitive compatibility condition for pasting DeltaY and DeltaR_h into one potential-outcome law and state necessity as well as sufficiency, or remove iff/frontier language from the introduction.

Strengthen the Hess/Jin comparison by stating exactly which MSM sharp-bound or robust-policy formula is not recovered except through the proposed normalization, since Hess already claims closed-form sharp bounds and optimal confounding-robust policy under MSM.

recommended_next_step: Revise the mathematical primitives for the CH--Riesz budget and delta-shift value functional, then restate Conjecture 2 as the promised iff compatibility frontier.
